ruleText: Priority among conflicting security interests is determined by the first to file or perfect.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Priority in Instruments
ruleText: A purchaser of an instrument who gives value and takes possession in good faith and without knowledge that the purchase violates another's rights may have priority over a perfected security interest perfected only by filing.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Priority in Investment Property
ruleText: A secured party who has control of investment property generally has priority over a secured party who does not have control.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Priority in Proceeds
ruleText: A perfected security interest in proceeds generally has the same date of priority as the perfected security interest in the original collateral so long as perfection in the proceeds continues.
buzzwords: same date of priority as original collateral
unsupported: same date of priority as original collateral

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Privileges and Immunities Clause — Article IV
ruleText: States may not discriminate against citizens of other states with respect to fundamental rights or important economic activities.

subject: Torts
title: Privileges in Defamation
ruleText: Absolute privileges protect statements in judicial, legislative, and certain executive proceedings, while qualified privileges protect statements made in good faith on proper occasions to proper recipients.

subject: Real Property
title: Privity — Tacking
ruleText: Privity exists when possession is transferred voluntarily between possessors.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Probate of a Lost or Destroyed Will
ruleText: A lost or destroyed will may be admitted to probate if the proponent proves due execution, the contents of the will, and that the will was not revoked, often by clear and convincing evidence.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Procedural Due Process — What Process Is Due
ruleText: The amount of process due depends on the private interest affected, the risk of erroneous deprivation, and the government’s interest.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Proceeds
ruleText: Proceeds include whatever is acquired upon the sale, lease, license, exchange, or other disposition of collateral; whatever is collected on or distributed on account of collateral; rights arising out of collateral; claims arising out of loss or damage to collateral; and insurance payable by reason of loss or damage to the collateral.

subject: Torts
title: Products Causation
ruleText: The plaintiff must prove the product defect actually and proximately caused the injury.

subject: Business Associations
title: Profit Sharing Presumption
ruleText: A person who receives a share of partnership profits is presumed to be a partner unless the profits were received in payment of a debt, wages, rent, interest on a loan, or for certain other exceptions.

subject: Business Associations
title: Profits and Losses
ruleText: Absent agreement otherwise, partners share profits equally and share losses in the same proportion as profits.

subject: Contracts
title: Promissory Estoppel
ruleText: A promise is enforceable under promissory estoppel when the promisor should reasonably expect reliance, the promise induces actual reliance, and injustice can be avoided only by enforcement.

subject: Contracts
title: Promissory Estoppel — Elements
ruleText: Promissory estoppel applies when a promisor should reasonably expect reliance, the promisee relies to their detriment, and injustice can be avoided only by enforcement of the promise.

subject: Business Associations
title: Promoter Fiduciary Duties
ruleText: A promoter owes fiduciary duties of good faith and fair dealing to the corporation and its investors, including the duty to disclose material facts and avoid secret profits from transactions involving the corporation.

subject: Business Associations
title: Promoter Liability on Preincorporation Contracts
ruleText: A promoter who enters a contract on behalf of a not-yet-formed corporation is personally liable on the contract unless the contract clearly provides otherwise or there is a later novation releasing the promoter.

subject: Family Law
title: Property Division — Equitable Distribution vs Community Property
ruleText: Equitable distribution divides marital property fairly but not necessarily equally, while community property states divide marital property equally.

subject: Family Law
title: Property Division — Marital vs Separate Property
ruleText: Marital property includes assets acquired during marriage, while separate property includes premarital assets, gifts, and inheritances unless transmuted.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Protected Interests — Property
ruleText: Property interests include legitimate claims of entitlement created by existing rules or understandings.

subject: Torts
title: Proximate Cause
ruleText: Proximate cause limits liability to those harms that are within the scope of the foreseeable risk created by the defendant's conduct.

subject: Business Associations
title: Proxy Voting
ruleText: A proxy is an authorization by a shareholder permitting another person to vote the shareholder's shares. Proxies are generally freely revocable unless coupled with an interest and stated to be irrevocable.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Public Forum — Limited or Nonpublic
ruleText: In limited or nonpublic forums, regulations must be reasonable and viewpoint neutral.

subject: Conflict of Laws
title: Public Policy Exception
ruleText: A forum may refuse to apply otherwise applicable foreign law when doing so would violate a strong public policy of the forum, but courts use this exception sparingly.

subject: Evidence
title: Public Records
ruleText: A record or statement of a public office is admissible if it sets out the office's activities, a matter observed while under a legal duty to report, or in a civil case or against the government in a criminal case factual findings from a legally authorized investigation, and the opponent does not show lack of trustworthiness.

subject: Evidence
topic: Contents of Writings, Recordings, and Photographs
title: Public Records and Summaries
ruleText: The contents of an official record may be proved by a copy certified as correct or testified to as correct by a witness who has compared it with the original. The contents of voluminous writings, recordings, or photographs that cannot be conveniently examined in court may be presented in the form of a summary, chart, or calculation if the originals or duplicates are made available for examination or copying.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Public Trial
ruleText: The Sixth Amendment generally guarantees a public criminal trial, though limited closures may be allowed if justified by overriding interests and narrowly tailored.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Purchase-Money Security Interest Defined
ruleText: A security interest is purchase-money to the extent that the goods are purchase-money collateral with respect to that security interest, meaning the obligation is incurred as all or part of the price of the collateral or for value given to enable the debtor to acquire rights in or use of the collateral, if the value is in fact so used.

subject: Torts
title: Pure Comparative Negligence
ruleText: Under pure comparative negligence, the plaintiff's recovery is reduced by the percentage of the plaintiff's fault.
buzzwords: reduced by the percentage of fault
unsupported: reduced by the percentage of fault

subject: Torts
title: Pure Economic Loss
ruleText: A plaintiff generally may not recover in ordinary negligence for purely economic loss unaccompanied by personal injury or property damage, absent a special relationship or recognized exception.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Purposeful Availment
ruleText: Minimum contacts require that the defendant purposefully avail themselves of the privilege of conducting activities in the forum.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Purposeful Availment
ruleText: Purposeful availment means the defendant deliberately engaged with the forum, invoking the benefits and protections of its laws, rather than being haled there by random, fortuitous, or unilateral acts.

subject: Family Law
title: Putative Spouse Doctrine
ruleText: A putative spouse is entitled to marital protections when a party has a good faith belief in the validity of the marriage.

subject: Real Property
title: Quitclaim Deed
ruleText: A quitclaim deed conveys whatever interest the grantor has without warranties of title.

subject: Real Property
title: Quitclaim Deed
ruleText: A quitclaim deed conveys whatever interest the grantor has, if any, without any covenants of title.

subject: Real Property
title: Race Recording Statute
ruleText: Under a race statute, the first party to record prevails regardless of notice.

subject: Real Property
title: Race Statute
ruleText: In a race jurisdiction, the first grantee to record prevails, regardless of notice.

subject: Real Property
title: Race-Notice Statute
ruleText: In a race-notice jurisdiction, a subsequent bona fide purchaser prevails only if the purchaser takes without notice and records first.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Racial Gerrymandering
ruleText: If race was the predominant factor in drawing district lines, the districting plan is subject to strict scrutiny.

subject: Evidence
subtopic: Relevance and Reasons for Excluding Relevant Evidence
title: Rape Shield Rule
ruleText: In civil and criminal proceedings involving alleged sexual misconduct, evidence offered to prove that a victim engaged in other sexual behavior or evidence offered to prove a victim's sexual predisposition is generally inadmissible, subject to limited exceptions.

subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
title: Ratification
ruleText: A principal ratifies an unauthorized act by affirming it after the fact or by accepting its benefits with knowledge of the material facts. Ratification retroactively creates the effect of authority if the principal had capacity and the act could have been authorized at the time.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Rational Basis — Default Rule
ruleText: Rational basis review applies to all classifications not subject to strict or intermediate scrutiny.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Rational Basis Review
ruleText: A law is upheld under rational basis review if it is rationally related to a legitimate governmental interest.

subject: Real Property
title: Real Covenant Benefit
ruleText: The benefit of a real covenant runs if there is a writing, intent, the covenant touches and concerns the land, and vertical privity in most jurisdictions.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Reasonable Description of Collateral
ruleText: A description of collateral is sufficient if it reasonably identifies what is described, including by specific listing, category, type of collateral defined in the UCC, quantity, computational formula, or any other method that objectively determines the collateral. But a supergeneric description such as 'all the debtor's assets' or 'all personal property' is generally insufficient in a security agreement.

subject: Torts
title: Recapture of Chattels
ruleText: A person may use reasonable force to recapture chattels if in fresh pursuit of one who has wrongfully taken them.

subject: Torts
title: Recapture of Chattels
ruleText: A person wrongfully dispossessed of chattels may use reasonable force in fresh pursuit after a timely demand for return, but not force if the property was lawfully acquired.

subject: Real Property
title: Reciprocal Negative Servitude
ruleText: Under a common development scheme, reciprocal negative servitudes may arise so that lot owners can enforce uniform restrictions against other lots if purchasers had notice of the scheme.

subject: Evidence
title: Refreshing Recollection
ruleText: A witness's recollection may be refreshed with almost any item, whether or not admissible, so long as the witness then testifies from present recollection. The adverse party is entitled to inspect the item, cross-examine the witness about it, and introduce relevant portions into evidence.

subject: Family Law
title: Registration for Enforcement
ruleText: A support order may be registered in another state for enforcement, and once confirmed, it is enforceable in the same manner as a local order, subject to limited defenses to registration.

subject: Evidence
title: Rehabilitation
ruleText: A witness may be rehabilitated after impeachment by evidence explaining or countering the impeachment.

subject: Evidence
title: Rehabilitation of Witnesses
ruleText: A witness may be rehabilitated after an attack on credibility, including by prior consistent statements when allowed, or by evidence of truthful character only after the witness's character for truthfulness has been attacked.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Reinitiation by Suspect
ruleText: After invoking Miranda rights, interrogation may resume only if the suspect initiates further communication.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Relation Back of Amendments
ruleText: An amendment relates back to the date of the original pleading if it arises out of the same conduct, transaction, or occurrence.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Relation Back When Changing Parties
ruleText: An amendment changing a party may relate back when it arises from the same conduct, transaction, or occurrence and the new party received timely notice and knew or should have known that but for a mistake it would have been named originally.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Reliability Factors
ruleText: Identification reliability is evaluated based on the witness’s opportunity to view, degree of attention, accuracy of prior description, level of certainty, and time between crime and identification.

subject: Contracts
title: Reliance Damages
ruleText: Reliance damages reimburse expenses incurred in reliance on the contract by putting the injured party in the position occupied before the contract was made.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Relief from Judgment
ruleText: A court may relieve a party from a final judgment or order for reasons such as mistake, excusable neglect, newly discovered evidence, fraud, or other grounds recognized by rule.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Relief from Judgment — Rule 60
ruleText: A court may grant relief from a final judgment for mistake, newly discovered evidence, fraud, or void judgments.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Religion Clauses — General Rule
ruleText: The First Amendment prohibits the government from establishing religion or prohibiting the free exercise of religion.

subject: Evidence
title: Religious Beliefs
ruleText: Evidence of a witness's religious beliefs or opinions is not admissible to attack or support the witness's credibility.

subject: Family Law
title: Relocation of Custodial Parent
ruleText: In relocation disputes, courts balance the relocating parent's reasons, the effect on the child's relationship with the other parent, the feasibility of preserving that relationship, and the child's best interests under the governing statute or case law.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Remedies for Breach of Trust
ruleText: For breach of trust, a court may compel performance, enjoin breaches, order redress by money damages or restoration of property, trace and recover trust assets, deny compensation, remove the trustee, or impose other equitable relief.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Remedy — Exclusion
ruleText: An identification obtained in violation of the right to counsel or due process is excluded from evidence.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Removal of Executive Officers
ruleText: The President generally may remove executive officers, but Congress may limit removal of certain inferior officers or members of independent regulatory commissions when such limits do not unduly interfere with executive power.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Removal of Trustee
ruleText: A court may remove a trustee for serious breach of trust, lack of cooperation impairing administration, unfitness, unwillingness, persistent failure to administer effectively, or substantial change of circumstances making removal in the beneficiaries' best interests.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Removal Power
ruleText: The President generally has the power to remove executive officers, subject to limited congressional restrictions.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Renewed JMOL
ruleText: A renewed motion for judgment as a matter of law may be made after trial if a prior JMOL motion was made.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Renewed Judgment as a Matter of Law
ruleText: A renewed motion for judgment as a matter of law may be made after verdict only if the party made a JMOL motion before the case was submitted to the jury.

subject: Real Property
title: Rent and Expenses Among Cotenants
ruleText: A cotenant in sole possession generally owes no rent to other cotenants absent ouster or agreement, but cotenants are entitled to contribution for necessary carrying costs such as taxes and mortgage interest, and may receive credits for repairs or improvements in partition.

subject: Conflict of Laws
title: Renvoi
ruleText: Renvoi is the doctrine under which a forum, when directed to apply another jurisdiction's law, may look to that jurisdiction's whole law including its conflict-of-laws rules, though most courts reject renvoi except in limited contexts.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Republication by Codicil
ruleText: A valid codicil republishes the will as of the date of the codicil, so the will is treated as reexecuted on that later date for many purposes.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Required Joinder
ruleText: A party must be joined if feasible when complete relief cannot be accorded without that party or the absent party claims an interest that may be impaired or may expose existing parties to multiple or inconsistent obligations.

subject: Torts
title: Rescue Doctrine
ruleText: If the defendant's negligence imperils a victim, the defendant is generally liable for injuries suffered by a foreseeable rescuer unless the rescuer acts rashly or recklessly.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Residence — Individuals
ruleText: For venue purposes, an individual resides in the judicial district where they are domiciled.

subject: Evidence
title: Residual Exception
ruleText: A hearsay statement not specifically covered by Rule 803 or 804 may be admitted if it has equivalent circumstantial guarantees of trustworthiness, is offered as evidence of a material fact, is more probative on the point than any other evidence that can be obtained through reasonable efforts, and admitting it will best serve the purposes of the rules and the interests of justice, with reasonable pretrial notice to the adverse party.

subject: Business Associations
title: Respondeat Superior
ruleText: An employer is vicariously liable for torts committed by an employee within the scope of employment under the doctrine of respondeat superior.

subject: Contracts
title: Restitution and Unjust Enrichment
ruleText: Restitution is available to prevent unjust enrichment when one party confers a benefit on another under circumstances making retention of the benefit unjust.

subject: Real Property
title: Retaliatory Eviction
ruleText: A landlord may not evict or otherwise retaliate against a residential tenant for reporting housing-code violations or exercising legal rights.

subject: Real Property
title: Reversion
ruleText: A reversion is a future interest retained by a grantor who conveys less than the grantor's entire estate.

subject: Trusts
title: Revival of Will
ruleText: A revoked will may be revived if the testator intends revival.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Revocable Trust
ruleText: Under modern law, a trust is presumed revocable unless the terms provide that it is irrevocable, although older law presumed the opposite in some jurisdictions.

subject: Trusts
title: Revocation — By Physical Act
ruleText: A will may be revoked by a physical act performed with intent to revoke.

subject: Trusts
title: Revocation — By Subsequent Writing
ruleText: A will may be revoked by a later will or codicil that expressly or impliedly revokes the prior will.

subject: Contracts
title: Revocation — General Rule
ruleText: An offeror may revoke an offer at any time before acceptance unless the offer is irrevocable.

subject: Contracts
title: Revocation — Indirect Revocation
ruleText: An offer is revoked indirectly when the offeree learns from a reliable source that the offeror has taken action inconsistent with the offer.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Revocation by Physical Act
ruleText: A will may be revoked by a subsequent physical act, such as burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating, or destroying, performed with the intent to revoke by the testator or at the testator's direction and in the testator's presence.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Revocation by Subsequent Instrument
ruleText: A will may be revoked by a later will or codicil executed with testamentary formalities if the later instrument expressly revokes the prior will or revokes it by inconsistency.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Right of Redemption
ruleText: A debtor, secondary obligor, or other secured party or lienholder may redeem collateral by tendering fulfillment of all obligations secured by the collateral and the reasonable expenses and attorney's fees of the secured party before the secured party has collected collateral, disposed of it, or entered into a contract for disposition, or before the secured party has accepted collateral in satisfaction of the debt.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Right to Confront Witnesses
ruleText: The Sixth Amendment guarantees the right to confront and cross-examine adverse witnesses.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Right to Jury Trial — Sixth Amendment
ruleText: The Sixth Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial in criminal prosecutions for serious offenses.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Right to Travel
ruleText: The Constitution protects the right of interstate travel, including the right to enter and leave states and to be treated equally as a new resident regarding fundamental matters.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Ripeness
ruleText: A claim is ripe when there is a present, concrete dispute and withholding review would create hardship, especially where further factual development is unnecessary.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Ripeness
ruleText: A claim is ripe when it presents a real and immediate dispute appropriate for judicial resolution.

subject: Real Property
title: Risk of Loss
ruleText: Under the traditional equitable conversion rule, the buyer bears the risk of casualty loss after contract formation unless the contract provides otherwise, though many modern statutes place the risk on the seller until possession or title passes.
buzzwords: seller until possession or title passes

subject: Contracts
title: Risk of Loss in Destination Contracts
ruleText: In a destination contract, risk of loss passes to the buyer when the goods are tendered at the destination.

subject: Contracts
title: Risk of Loss in Shipment Contracts
ruleText: In a shipment contract, risk of loss passes to the buyer when the seller duly delivers the goods to the carrier.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Rule 12(b) Motions — Overview
ruleText: Rule 12(b) permits defenses to be raised by motion before filing an answer.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Rule 12(b)(2) — Personal Jurisdiction
ruleText: Lack of personal jurisdiction must be raised in the first Rule 12 response or it is waived.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Rule 12(b)(3) — Improper Venue
ruleText: Improper venue must be raised in the first Rule 12 response or it is waived.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Rule 12(f) — Motion to Strike
ruleText: A court may strike insufficient defenses or redundant, immaterial, or scandalous matter.

subject: Evidence
title: Rule 403 — Balancing Test
ruleText: Relevant evidence may be excluded if its probative value is substantially outweighed by the danger of unfair prejudice, confusion, or misleading the jury.

subject: Evidence
title: Rule 403 Balancing
ruleText: Relevant evidence may be excluded if its probative value is substantially outweighed by a danger of unfair prejudice, confusing the issues, misleading the jury, undue delay, wasting time, or needlessly presenting cumulative evidence.

subject: Real Property
title: Rule Against Perpetuities — General Rule
ruleText: A future interest is invalid unless it must vest, if at all, within 21 years after a life in being at the creation of the interest.

subject: Contracts
title: Rules of Contract Interpretation
ruleText: Contracts are interpreted to give effect to the parties’ intent as expressed in the contract language.

subject: Business Associations
title: Sale of Substantially All Assets
ruleText: A sale, lease, exchange, or other disposition of all or substantially all corporate assets outside the usual and regular course of business generally requires board approval and shareholder approval, while ordinary-course dispositions usually do not.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Same Offense — Blockburger Test
ruleText: Two offenses are the same offense for double jeopardy purposes unless each offense requires proof of a fact the other does not.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: School and Workplace Searches
ruleText: Certain public school and government workplace searches may be judged by reasonableness rather than probable cause and warrant requirements.

subject: Evidence
title: Scope of Cross-Examination
ruleText: Cross-examination should not go beyond the subject matter of the direct examination and matters affecting the witness's credibility, although the court may allow inquiry into additional matters as if on direct examination.

subject: Real Property
title: Scope of Easement
ruleText: The scope of an easement is limited to the terms of its creation.

subject: Business Associations
title: Scope of Employment
ruleText: Conduct is within the scope of employment if it is of the kind the employee was employed to perform, occurs substantially within authorized time and space limits, and is motivated at least in part by a purpose to serve the employer.

subject: Torts
title: Scope of Employment
ruleText: Conduct falls within the scope of employment when it is of the kind the employee was hired to perform, occurs substantially within authorized time and space limits, and is motivated at least in part to serve the employer.

subject: Torts
title: Scope of Risk
ruleText: Strict liability applies only to harm that results from the type of risk that makes the activity abnormally dangerous.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Search — Physical Intrusion
ruleText: A search occurs when the government physically intrudes into a constitutionally protected area for the purpose of obtaining information.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Search Definition
ruleText: A search occurs when the government intrudes on a reasonable expectation of privacy or physically trespasses on a constitutionally protected area to obtain information.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Search Incident to Arrest — Vehicle
ruleText: Police may search a vehicle incident to arrest only if the arrestee is unsecured and within reaching distance or if evidence of the crime of arrest may be found in the vehicle.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Search Incident to Lawful Arrest — Person
ruleText: Police may search the arrestee’s person incident to a lawful arrest without a warrant.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Search Warrant — Third Party Home
ruleText: Police may not enter a third party’s home to execute an arrest warrant without a search warrant or consent.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Section 5 of the Fourteenth Amendment
ruleText: Congress may enforce the Fourteenth Amendment by adopting legislation that is congruent and proportional to constitutional violations by state actors.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Secured Party Purchase at Sale
ruleText: A secured party may buy collateral at a public disposition, and may buy at a private disposition only if the collateral is of a kind customarily sold on a recognized market or the subject of widely distributed standard price quotations.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Security Agreement Requirements
ruleText: Unless possession or control substitutes for a signed record, a security agreement must be authenticated by the debtor and must reasonably identify the collateral.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Security Interest
ruleText: A security interest is an interest in personal property or fixtures that secures payment or performance of an obligation.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Seizure Definition
ruleText: A person is seized when a reasonable person would not feel free to leave due to government force or show of authority, and the person submits or is physically restrained.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Seizure of Person
ruleText: A person is seized when, under the totality of circumstances, a reasonable person would not feel free to leave.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Seizure of Property
ruleText: Property is seized when the government meaningfully interferes with an individual’s possessory interest.

subject: Evidence
title: Self-Authenticating Evidence
ruleText: Certain items require no extrinsic evidence of authenticity, including sealed and signed public documents, certified public records, official publications, newspapers and periodicals, trade inscriptions, acknowledged documents, commercial paper, and certain certified electronic records.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Self-Defense
ruleText: A person may use reasonable nondeadly force when reasonably necessary to defend against imminent unlawful force, and deadly force when reasonably necessary to prevent imminent death or serious bodily harm.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Self-Defense — Duty to Retreat
ruleText: A defendant must retreat before using deadly force if retreat can be accomplished safely, unless in the defendant’s home.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Self-Settled Trust
ruleText: Under the traditional rule, if the settlor creates a trust for the settlor's own benefit, the settlor's creditors can generally reach the maximum amount that could be distributed to or for the settlor's benefit, regardless of spendthrift language, subject to domestic asset-protection trust statutes in a few jurisdictions.

subject: Contracts
title: Seller's Market Damages
ruleText: A seller may recover the difference between the market price at the time and place for tender and the unpaid contract price, plus incidental damages, less expenses saved.

subject: Contracts
title: Seller's Resale Damages
ruleText: A seller who resells in good faith and in a commercially reasonable manner may recover the difference between the resale price and contract price plus incidental damages, less expenses saved.
buzzwords: difference between resale and contract price
unsupported: difference between resale and contract price

subject: Evidence
title: Sensory or Mental Defect
ruleText: A witness may be impeached by showing defects in perception, memory, or narration, including impaired vision, hearing, intoxication, or mental condition affecting the ability to observe or recall.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Separation of Powers — General Principle
ruleText: The Constitution divides federal power among the legislative, executive, and judicial branches to prevent the concentration of power.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Serious vs Petty Offenses
ruleText: An offense is considered serious if the authorized punishment exceeds six months of imprisonment.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Service of Process
ruleText: Proper service of process requires delivery of the summons and complaint by a method authorized by rule and reasonably calculated to notify the defendant.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Seventh Amendment Jury Trial Right
ruleText: The Seventh Amendment preserves the right to a jury trial in federal civil actions for legal claims, but not for purely equitable claims.

subject: Real Property
title: Severance of Joint Tenancy
ruleText: A joint tenancy is severed when one joint tenant destroys one of the four unities, such as by conveying that tenant's interest to a third party.

subject: Real Property
title: Severance of Joint Tenancy
ruleText: A joint tenancy is severed when one co-tenant transfers their interest, destroying the unities.

subject: Business Associations
title: Shareholder Meetings
ruleText: Corporations generally must hold annual shareholder meetings, and special meetings may be called by persons authorized by statute, the articles, or bylaws. Notice must state the time and place and, for special meetings, often the purpose.

subject: Business Associations
title: Shareholder Quorum and Voting
ruleText: A majority of the votes entitled to be cast on a matter generally constitutes a quorum for shareholder action, and if a quorum exists, action is approved if the votes cast in favor exceed votes cast against, unless the articles, bylaws, or statute require a greater vote.
buzzwords: majority of votes entitled to be cast
unsupported: majority of votes entitled to be cast

subject: Business Associations
title: Shareholders — Derivative Actions
ruleText: A shareholder may bring a derivative action on behalf of the corporation after making a demand on the board or showing demand futility.

subject: Business Associations
title: Shareholders — Voting and Management
ruleText: Shareholders elect directors and vote on fundamental changes but do not manage daily corporate affairs.

subject: Real Property
title: Shelter Rule
ruleText: A transferee from a bona fide purchaser takes shelter in the transferor's status and receives the same priority, even if the transferee had notice.
buzzwords: transferee from a bona fide purchaser

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Slayer Rule
ruleText: A person who feloniously and intentionally kills the decedent is barred from taking from the decedent's estate, and the estate is distributed as though the killer predeceased the decedent.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Solicitation
ruleText: Solicitation is asking, enticing, counseling, or commanding another to commit a crime with the intent that the crime be committed.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Solicitation — Completion
ruleText: Solicitation is complete once the request or encouragement is made, regardless of whether the crime is committed.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Sources of Legal Duty
ruleText: Legal duties arise from statute, contract, special relationship, voluntary assumption of care that secludes the victim, or creation of the peril.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Special Needs Searches
ruleText: Searches conducted for needs beyond ordinary law enforcement may be reasonable without individualized suspicion under balancing analysis.

subject: Real Property
title: Special Warranty Deed
ruleText: A special warranty deed provides warranties only against defects arising during the grantor’s ownership.

subject: Evidence
title: Specific Acts on Cross-Examination
ruleText: Except for criminal convictions under Rule 609, extrinsic evidence is not admissible to prove specific instances of a witness's conduct in order to attack or support the witness's character for truthfulness. But on cross-examination the court may allow inquiry into specific instances if they are probative of the character for truthfulness or untruthfulness of the witness or another witness whose character the witness being cross-examined has testified about.

subject: Contracts
title: Specific Performance
ruleText: Specific performance is available when legal damages are inadequate, such as for unique goods or real property, and equitable relief is otherwise appropriate.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Spending Power and Conditional Grants
ruleText: Congress may condition federal grants on compliance with federal requirements if the spending is for the general welfare, conditions are unambiguous, related to the program, not independently unconstitutional, and not unduly coercive.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Spendthrift Trust
ruleText: A spendthrift clause restrains both voluntary and involuntary transfer of a beneficiary's interest, preventing the beneficiary from assigning it and generally preventing most creditors from reaching it before distribution.

subject: Family Law
title: Spousal Support (Alimony)
ruleText: Spousal support may be awarded and modified based on factors including need, ability to pay, duration of marriage, and a substantial change in circumstances.

subject: Family Law
title: Spousal Support Factors
ruleText: Courts consider factors such as need, ability to pay, duration of the marriage, standard of living during marriage, age and health, earning capacities, contributions to the marriage, and the time needed for education or training.

subject: Evidence
title: Spousal Testimonial Privilege
ruleText: In criminal cases, the witness-spouse may refuse to testify adversely against the defendant-spouse during the marriage. The privilege belongs to the witness-spouse and ends upon divorce.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Standing — No Vicarious Standing
ruleText: A defendant may not challenge a search or seizure based solely on the violation of another person’s rights.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Standing — Personal Rights
ruleText: A defendant may invoke the exclusionary rule only if the defendant’s own Fourth Amendment rights were violated.

subject: Torts
title: Standing — Public Nuisance
ruleText: A private individual may recover for public nuisance only if the individual suffers a special injury different in kind from the general public.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Standing — Redressability
ruleText: Redressability requires that a favorable court decision is likely to remedy the injury.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Standing to Challenge Search
ruleText: Only a person whose own reasonable expectation of privacy was violated may seek suppression under the Fourth Amendment.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: State Action Requirement
ruleText: The Equal Protection Clause applies only to government action and not to purely private conduct.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: State Action Requirement — Speech
ruleText: The First Amendment applies only to government action and not to private conduct.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: State Officers and Personal Liability
ruleText: State officers may be sued in their personal capacity for damages, subject to personal immunities, because such suits are not treated as suits against the state.

subject: Evidence
title: Statement Against Interest
ruleText: A statement against interest is admissible when a declarant makes a statement so contrary to the declarant’s pecuniary, proprietary, or penal interest that a reasonable person would not have made it unless true.

subject: Evidence
title: Statement Against Interest
ruleText: A statement that a reasonable person in the declarant's position would have made only if the person believed it to be true because, when made, it was so contrary to the declarant's proprietary or pecuniary interest or had so great a tendency to invalidate the declarant's claim or to expose the declarant to civil or criminal liability is admissible if the declarant is unavailable. In a criminal case, a statement tending to expose the declarant to criminal liability and offered to exculpate the accused requires corroborating circumstances that clearly indicate trustworthiness.

subject: Evidence
title: Statement of an Opposing Party
ruleText: A statement is defined as not hearsay if it is offered against an opposing party and was made by the party in an individual or representative capacity; is one the party manifested that it adopted or believed true; was made by a person whom the party authorized to make a statement on the subject; was made by the party's agent or employee on a matter within the scope of that relationship and while it existed; or was made by the party's coconspirator during and in furtherance of the conspiracy.

subject: Evidence
title: Statement of Personal or Family History
ruleText: Statements concerning a declarant’s own birth, marriage, divorce, ancestry, or similar family history are admissible when the declarant is unavailable.

subject: Evidence
title: Statements for Medical Diagnosis or Treatment
ruleText: Statements made for purposes of medical diagnosis or treatment describing medical history, symptoms, or cause are admissible.

subject: Contracts
title: Statute of Frauds
ruleText: Certain contracts are unenforceable unless evidenced by a writing signed by the party to be charged.
buzzwords: signed by the party to be charged

subject: Real Property
title: Statute of Frauds
ruleText: A contract for the sale of an interest in land must generally be in writing, signed by the party to be charged, and contain the essential terms.

subject: Contracts
title: Statute of Frauds — Equitable Estoppel
ruleText: A party may be estopped from asserting the Statute of Frauds when reliance makes enforcement necessary to prevent injustice.

subject: Real Property
title: Statute of Frauds — Essential Terms
ruleText: The writing must identify the parties, describe the land, and state the price.

subject: Contracts
title: Statute of Frauds — General Rule
ruleText: Certain contracts must be in writing and signed by the party to be charged to be enforceable.

subject: Real Property
title: Statute of Frauds — Land Contracts
ruleText: A contract for the sale of land must be in writing and signed by the party to be charged.

subject: Contracts
title: Statute of Frauds — Suretyship (Guarantee)
ruleText: The Statute of Frauds applies to a promise to answer for the debt or duty of another.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Statutory Rape
ruleText: Statutory rape is sexual intercourse with a person below the age of consent, and mistake as to age is often not a defense.

subject: Real Property
title: Statutory Right of Redemption
ruleText: Some states allow redemption after foreclosure by statute.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Stop and Frisk — Frisk
ruleText: Police may frisk a detainee if they reasonably suspect the person is armed and dangerous.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Stop and Frisk — Terry Stop
ruleText: Police may briefly detain a person if they have reasonable suspicion of criminal activity.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Strict Foreclosure
ruleText: A secured party may accept collateral in full or partial satisfaction of the obligation if the debtor consents after default and other statutory conditions are met, including sending proposal notices to certain interested parties and receiving no timely objection. In consumer transactions, acceptance in partial satisfaction is generally not allowed.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Strict Per Stirpes
ruleText: Under strict per stirpes, the estate is divided into shares at the first generational level below the decedent regardless of whether anyone there survives, and descendants of a deceased child take that child's share by representation.

subject: Torts
title: Strict Products Liability
ruleText: The plaintiff must show the defendant is a commercial supplier, the product was defective, the defect existed when it left the defendant's control, and the defect actually and proximately caused the plaintiff's injury while the product was being used in a foreseeable manner.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Strict Scrutiny
ruleText: Under strict scrutiny, the government must show the law is narrowly tailored to achieve a compelling governmental interest.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Strict Scrutiny — Suspect Classifications
ruleText: Strict scrutiny applies to classifications based on race, national origin, and alienage.

subject: Real Property
title: Sublease
ruleText: A sublease transfers less than the tenant’s entire remaining interest.

subject: Business Associations
title: Subscriptions for Shares
ruleText: A preincorporation subscription is an offer to buy stock in the future corporation and is often irrevocable for the period specified by statute unless all subscribers agree to revoke.

subject: Contracts
title: Subsequent Illegality
ruleText: A contract is discharged when performance becomes illegal due to a change in law after formation.

subject: Evidence
subtopic: Relevance and Reasons for Excluding Relevant Evidence
title: Subsequent Remedial Measures
ruleText: Evidence of measures that would have made an earlier injury or harm less likely to occur is not admissible to prove negligence, culpable conduct, a defect in a product or its design, or a need for a warning or instruction. But it may be admissible for another purpose, such as impeachment or proving ownership, control, or the feasibility of precautionary measures if disputed.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Substance Over Form
ruleText: Article 9 looks to the economic substance of a transaction rather than its label, and a transaction that in substance creates a security interest will be governed as a secured transaction even if the parties label it as a lease, consignment, sale, or another arrangement.

subject: Conflict of Laws
title: Substance Versus Procedure
ruleText: The forum generally applies its own procedural rules, but it may apply another state's substantive law when choice-of-law rules point to that law.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Substantial Effects on Interstate Commerce
ruleText: Congress may regulate economic or commercial intrastate activity that, in the aggregate, substantially affects interstate commerce.

subject: Contracts
title: Substantial Performance
ruleText: Substantial performance occurs when a party performs in good faith and any breach is minor rather than material.

subject: Contracts
title: Substantial Performance
ruleText: Under common law, substantial performance satisfies the contract and triggers the other party's duty to perform, subject to damages for the defects.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Substantive Due Process — General Rule
ruleText: Substantive due process limits the government’s ability to infringe on fundamental rights regardless of the procedures used.

subject: Torts
title: Superseding Cause
ruleText: An intervening cause becomes superseding when it is so unforeseeable that it breaks the chain of proximate causation and relieves the defendant of liability for subsequent harm.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Supplemental Jurisdiction — Discretionary Decline
ruleText: A court may decline supplemental jurisdiction if the claim raises novel state issues, substantially predominates, or if the original claims are dismissed.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Supplemental Jurisdiction Limitation in Diversity Cases
ruleText: In diversity cases, supplemental jurisdiction generally cannot be used to override complete diversity for certain claims by plaintiffs against persons made parties under the federal joinder rules.

subject: Family Law
title: Support and Visitation Are Independent
ruleText: A parent's duty to pay child support is independent of the other parent's duty to permit visitation, so denial of visitation generally does not excuse support payments, and nonpayment does not justify denying visitation absent court order.
buzzwords: support not excused by denied visitation
unsupported: support not excused by denied visitation

subject: Real Property
title: Surrender
ruleText: Surrender is an agreement between landlord and tenant that the tenant will give up the leasehold before the term ends, terminating future obligations.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Surviving Spouse's Share in Intestacy
ruleText: A surviving spouse's intestate share depends on the statutory scheme and whether the decedent is survived by descendants or parents, with the spouse often taking all if all surviving descendants are also descendants of the spouse and no other descendants complicate the distribution.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Suspect Classifications
ruleText: Race, national origin, and classifications affecting some noncitizens are suspect and generally trigger strict scrutiny.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Symbolic Speech
ruleText: Symbolic conduct is protected as speech if the conduct is intended to convey a message and the message is likely to be understood.

subject: Real Property
title: Tacking
ruleText: Successive possessors may tack periods of possession if there is privity between them.

subject: Real Property
title: Tacking
ruleText: Successive adverse possessors may tack their periods of possession if there is privity between them, such as by voluntary transfer.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Taxing Power
ruleText: Congress may tax and spend for the general welfare.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Taxpayer Standing
ruleText: A taxpayer generally lacks standing to challenge government expenditures, except a federal taxpayer may challenge a congressional spending program alleged to violate the Establishment Clause under the narrow Flast exception.

subject: Family Law
title: Temporary Emergency Jurisdiction
ruleText: A court may exercise temporary emergency jurisdiction if the child is present in the state and the child has been abandoned or needs emergency protection because the child, a sibling, or a parent is subjected to or threatened with mistreatment or abuse.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Temporary Perfection — Proceeds
ruleText: A perfected security interest in proceeds remains perfected for 20 days.

subject: Real Property
title: Temporary Taking
ruleText: A temporary regulation that denies use of property may constitute a compensable taking.

subject: Real Property
title: Tenancy at Sufferance
ruleText: A tenancy at sufferance arises when a tenant wrongfully remains in possession after lease termination.

subject: Real Property
title: Tenancy by the Entirety
ruleText: Tenancy by the entirety is a marital concurrent estate with right of survivorship in which neither spouse acting alone may sever or convey the estate in most jurisdictions.

subject: Real Property
title: Tenancy for Years
ruleText: A tenancy for years lasts for a fixed, definite period of time and terminates automatically.

subject: Real Property
title: Tenancy in Common
ruleText: A tenancy in common is a concurrent estate in which each cotenant owns an undivided fractional interest that is freely transferable, devisable, and descendible, and there is no right of survivorship.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Tenth Amendment
ruleText: The Tenth Amendment reserves to the states powers not delegated to the federal government.

subject: Business Associations
title: Termination of Actual Authority
ruleText: Actual authority terminates by revocation by the principal, renunciation by the agent, occurrence of circumstances causing the agent reasonably to believe the principal no longer consents, completion of the task, lapse of time, or death or loss of capacity of the principal or agent.

subject: Business Associations
title: Termination of Apparent Authority
ruleText: Apparent authority terminates when it is no longer reasonable for the third party to believe the agent is authorized, usually after notice from the principal or other facts that make reliance unreasonable.

subject: Real Property
title: Termination of Covenants and Servitudes
ruleText: Covenants and servitudes may terminate by release, merger, changed conditions, or abandonment.

subject: Real Property
title: Termination of Easement
ruleText: An easement may be terminated by merger, release, abandonment, prescription, or necessity ending.

subject: Contracts
title: Termination of Offer — Methods
ruleText: An offer may be terminated by revocation, rejection, counteroffer, lapse of time, or death or incapacity of either party.

subject: Family Law
title: Termination of Parental Rights
ruleText: Parental rights may be terminated only upon proof of statutory grounds such as abuse, neglect, abandonment, prolonged failure to support, or unfitness, along with compliance with due process and, typically, a best-interests determination.

subject: Family Law
title: Termination of Parental Rights
ruleText: Parental rights may be terminated upon clear and convincing evidence of abuse, neglect, abandonment, or unfitness.

subject: Contracts
title: Third Party Beneficiary
ruleText: A third party may enforce a contract when the contract was intended to benefit that third party and the third party's rights have vested.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Third Party Standing
ruleText: A litigant generally may not raise the rights of third parties, but may do so if there is a close relationship and a hindrance to the third party's ability to protect their own interests.

subject: Family Law
title: Third-Party Custody
ruleText: Because a legal parent has a fundamental right to custody, a third party usually may obtain custody only by rebutting the parental preference through proof such as unfitness, extraordinary circumstances, or serious detriment to the child under applicable law.

subject: Real Property
title: Time Is of the Essence
ruleText: Time is not of the essence in land contracts unless expressly stated.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Timing — JMOL
ruleText: A motion for judgment as a matter of law must be made before the case is submitted to the jury.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Timing of Hearing
ruleText: A hearing may be held after deprivation if prompt post-deprivation review is provided and pre-deprivation process is impracticable.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Timing of Summary Judgment
ruleText: A party may move for summary judgment after 20 days from commencement of the action or after service of a motion for summary judgment.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Trade Name
ruleText: A financing statement that provides only the debtor's trade name does not sufficiently provide the debtor's name.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Traditional Bases of Personal Jurisdiction
ruleText: Traditional bases of personal jurisdiction include service on the defendant while present in the forum, domicile in the forum, consent, and in some cases waiver.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Traditional Bases of Personal Jurisdiction
ruleText: Personal jurisdiction exists where the defendant is domiciled in the forum, personally served in the forum, or consents to jurisdiction.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Transactions Excluded from Article 9
ruleText: Article 9 generally does not apply to most landlord's liens, statutory liens other than agricultural liens, assignments of wages, transfers of interests in real property except fixtures and certain related collateral, tort claims other than commercial tort claims, and many transfers by operation of law.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Transfer — Improper Venue (1406)
ruleText: When venue is improper, the transferee court applies its own choice-of-law rules.

subject: Business Associations
title: Transfer of LLC Interests
ruleText: Absent agreement otherwise, a member may transfer the member's transferable interest, which generally consists of economic rights to distributions, but the transferee does not become a member or obtain management rights without the consent required by statute or the operating agreement.

subject: Business Associations
title: Transfer of Partnership Interest
ruleText: A partner may transfer the partner's transferable interest, which generally consists of the partner's share of profits, losses, and distributions, but a transferee does not become a partner or acquire management rights without consent of the other partners.

subject: Real Property
title: Transfer of the Note
ruleText: The mortgage generally follows the note, so transfer of the debt ordinarily transfers the security interest as well.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Transfer or Dismiss When Venue Is Wrong
ruleText: When venue is improper, the court may dismiss or transfer the case to a proper district if justice so requires.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Transfer to New Debtor in Another State
ruleText: If collateral is transferred to a new debtor located in another jurisdiction and the security interest becomes perfected against the new debtor under the law of that jurisdiction, the security interest generally remains perfected for one year after the transfer or until perfection would otherwise lapse, whichever occurs earlier, unless reperfected sooner under the new jurisdiction's law.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Transferee of Collateral
ruleText: A security interest generally follows the collateral into the hands of a transferee unless a rule cuts it off, such as buyer-in-ordinary-course protection, authorized disposition, consumer-buyer protection, or another statutory exception.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Transferred Intent
ruleText: Transferred intent applies when a defendant intends to harm one victim but accidentally harms another.

subject: Family Law
title: Transmutation
ruleText: Transmutation occurs when separate property is converted into marital or community property, or vice versa, by agreement, titling, gift, or conduct showing intent to change the property's character.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Treaty Power
ruleText: The President negotiates treaties, which become effective with approval by two-thirds of the Senate; valid treaties are supreme law, subject to the Constitution.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Trial — Right to Jury Trial
ruleText: The Seventh Amendment preserves the right to a jury trial in civil actions at law.

subject: Trusts
title: Trust Modification — Cy Pres
ruleText: Cy pres allows modification of a charitable trust to approximate the settlor’s intent.

subject: Trusts
title: Trust Termination
ruleText: A trust may terminate when its purpose is fulfilled or becomes impossible.

subject: Conflict of Laws
title: Trust Validity and Administration
ruleText: Trust validity and administration may be governed by the law designated in the trust instrument or, absent a valid designation, by the law of the jurisdiction with the most significant relationship to the trust or its administration.

subject: Trusts
title: Trustee Duties — Care
ruleText: A trustee must administer the trust with reasonable care and skill.

subject: Trusts
title: Trustee Duties — Impartiality
ruleText: A trustee must act impartially among beneficiaries.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Trustee Duty of Loyalty
ruleText: A trustee must administer the trust solely in the interests of the beneficiaries and avoid self-dealing, conflicts of interest, and transactions affected by personal interests unless properly authorized or consented to.
buzzwords: solely in the interests of beneficiaries
unsupported: solely in the interests of beneficiaries

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Trustee Duty of Prudence
ruleText: A trustee must administer the trust with the care, skill, and caution that a prudent person would use in similar circumstances, taking into account the purposes, terms, and beneficiaries of the trust.

subject: Family Law
title: Types of Spousal Support
ruleText: Common forms of spousal support include temporary, rehabilitative, reimbursement, permanent or long-term periodic, and lump-sum support, depending on statutory standards and the parties' circumstances.

subject: Contracts
title: UCC Contract Modification
ruleText: Under the UCC, consideration is not required to modify a contract for the sale of goods if the modification is made in good faith.

subject: Contracts
title: UCC Statute of Frauds — Admission Exception
ruleText: The Statute of Frauds is satisfied to the extent that a party admits in court that a contract for sale was made.

subject: Contracts
title: UCC Statute of Frauds — Specially Manufactured Goods
ruleText: The Statute of Frauds does not apply when goods are specially manufactured for the buyer and are not suitable for sale to others.

subject: Contracts
title: UCC Statute of Frauds Exceptions
ruleText: UCC exceptions include specially manufactured goods, admissions in pleadings or testimony, and goods paid for or accepted.

subject: Conflict of Laws
title: UIFSA and Child Support
ruleText: Interstate child-support enforcement and modification are generally governed by UIFSA, which seeks to ensure one controlling support order and continuing exclusive jurisdiction in the proper issuing state.

subject: Family Law
title: UIFSA and Interstate Support
ruleText: UIFSA governs establishment, enforcement, and modification of child-support orders across state lines by providing rules for issuing, registering, enforcing, and determining controlling support orders.

subject: Evidence
title: Ultimate Issue
ruleText: Opinion testimony is not objectionable just because it embraces an ultimate issue, but in a criminal case an expert may not state an opinion about whether the defendant did or did not have a mental state or condition that constitutes an element of the crime or defense.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Unanimity Rule for Removal
ruleText: All properly joined and served defendants generally must join in or consent to removal.
buzzwords: join in or consent to removal

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Undue Influence
ruleText: A will or provision is invalid for undue influence if the influencer exerted such pressure that it overcame the testator's free will and caused a disposition the testator otherwise would not have made.

subject: Real Property
title: Unmarketable Title — Defects
ruleText: Title is unmarketable if it is subject to encumbrances, adverse possession claims, or zoning violations.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Unperfected Versus Unperfected
ruleText: As between conflicting unperfected security interests, priority generally goes to the first to attach or become enforceable.

subject: Torts
title: Unreasonableness — Balancing Test
ruleText: Unreasonableness in nuisance is determined by balancing the gravity of the harm against the utility of the defendant’s conduct.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Use of Illegally Obtained Evidence — Impeachment
ruleText: Illegally obtained evidence may be used to impeach the defendant’s testimony but not to impeach other witnesses.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Use of Statements — Case-in-Chief
ruleText: Statements obtained in violation of Miranda are inadmissible in the prosecution’s case-in-chief.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Use of Statements — Impeachment
ruleText: Statements obtained in violation of Miranda may be used to impeach the defendant’s testimony if voluntary.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Use of Statements — Sixth Amendment Violation
ruleText: Statements obtained in violation of the Sixth Amendment right to counsel are inadmissible in the prosecution’s case-in-chief.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Use of Statements. — Impeachment
ruleText: Statements obtained in violation of the Sixth Amendment may be used to impeach the defendant’s testimony if voluntary.

subject: Evidence
title: Use of Writings on Cross
ruleText: When examining a witness about a prior statement or other writing, the examiner need not show it or disclose its contents to the witness at that time, but on request must show it or disclose its contents to opposing counsel.
buzzwords: must show opposing counsel on request
unsupported: must show opposing counsel on request

subject: Conflict of Laws
title: Usury and Contracts
ruleText: Courts often uphold a contract against a usury challenge if the contract bears a reasonable relationship to a jurisdiction whose law would validate the interest rate, unless doing so would violate a strong public policy of the forum.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Vagueness
ruleText: A law is void for vagueness if it fails to give reasonable notice of what is prohibited or invites arbitrary and discriminatory enforcement.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Vagueness Doctrine
ruleText: A law is unconstitutionally vague if it fails to give fair notice of prohibited conduct or encourages arbitrary enforcement.

subject: Family Law
title: Validity of Premarital Agreements
ruleText: A premarital agreement is generally enforceable if entered voluntarily, after fair and reasonable disclosure or waiver of disclosure, and if the agreement is not unconscionable under applicable law.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Vehicle Search Incident to Arrest
ruleText: Police may search a vehicle incident to arrest if the arrestee is unsecured and within reaching distance of the passenger compartment, or if it is reasonable to believe evidence of the offense of arrest might be found inside.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Venue — Multiple Defendants
ruleText: When all defendants reside in the same state, venue is proper in any district where any defendant resides.

subject: Real Property
title: Vested Remainder
ruleText: A remainder is vested if it is given to an ascertained person and is not subject to a condition precedent other than the natural termination of the preceding estate.

subject: Real Property
title: Vested Remainder Subject to Open
ruleText: A vested remainder subject to open is a vested remainder held by at least one ascertained member of a class that may increase in size.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Void or Invalid Gifts
ruleText: If a gift is void or invalid, the property usually falls into the residue, or passes by intestacy if the residue does not dispose of it.

subject: Family Law
title: Voidable Marriage
ruleText: A marriage is voidable when a defect such as fraud, duress, temporary incapacity, or nonage makes the marriage subject to challenge by a proper party, but the marriage remains valid unless and until annulled.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Voluntariness of Confessions
ruleText: A confession is involuntary if police coercion, viewed under the totality of circumstances, overbears the defendant's will.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Voluntary Act
ruleText: Criminal liability requires a voluntary bodily movement or a qualifying omission where there is a legal duty to act.

subject: Business Associations
title: Voting Trusts
ruleText: A voting trust is an arrangement by which shareholders transfer legal title of their shares to a trustee who votes the shares pursuant to the trust agreement, while the beneficial owners retain economic interests.

subject: Evidence
title: Waiver of Attorney-Client Privilege
ruleText: The attorney-client privilege may be waived by voluntary disclosure of the privileged communication to a third party, by failure to maintain confidentiality, or by placing the communication at issue.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Waiver of Rule 12 Defenses
ruleText: Certain Rule 12 defenses are waived if not raised in the first Rule 12 response.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Waiver of Service
ruleText: A defendant may waive service of process by returning a waiver request, avoiding formal service.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Waiver of Service
ruleText: A defendant who waives formal service does not waive objections to venue or jurisdiction and receives additional time to respond.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: War and Related Powers
ruleText: Congress has powers to declare war, raise and support armies, provide for the navy, call forth and regulate the militia, and make rules concerning captures and military governance.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Warrant — Particularity Requirement
ruleText: A warrant must particularly describe the place to be searched and the persons or things to be seized.
buzzwords: persons or things to be seized

subject: Real Property
title: Waste
ruleText: Waste is conduct by a holder of a possessory estate, such as a life tenant, that unreasonably harms the interests of future interest holders.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Wealth and Education
ruleText: Wealth is generally not a suspect classification and education is not a fundamental right, so ordinary distinctions in school funding usually receive rational basis review.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Well-Pleaded Complaint Rule
ruleText: Federal question jurisdiction must appear on the face of the plaintiff’s well-pleaded complaint.

subject: Evidence
title: What Counts as a Statement
ruleText: A statement is a person's oral assertion, written assertion, or nonverbal conduct if the person intended it as an assertion.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: When a Required Party Cannot Be Joined
ruleText: If a required party cannot be joined, the court decides whether to proceed without that party or dismiss the action after considering fairness and practical factors.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Where to File
ruleText: Except for certain fixture filings, timber to be cut, and as-extracted collateral, a financing statement is generally filed in the central filing office of the debtor's location.

subject: Real Property
title: Wild Deed
ruleText: A wild deed is a recorded deed not connected to the chain of title, and it generally does not give record notice to later purchasers.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
title: Withdrawal by Accomplice
ruleText: An accomplice withdraws by repudiating prior aid, attempting to neutralize assistance, and timely communicating withdrawal before the crime is committed.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Work Product Doctrine
ruleText: Materials prepared in anticipation of litigation are protected from discovery unless substantial need and undue hardship are shown.

subject: Business Associations
title: Wrongful Dissociation
ruleText: A partner's dissociation is wrongful if it breaches an express term of the partnership agreement or occurs before expiration of a term or completion of an undertaking in certain ways, such as wrongful withdrawal or bankruptcy where treated as wrongful by statute or agreement.

subject: Real Property
title: Zoning — Use Variance vs Area Variance
ruleText: A use variance permits a prohibited land use, while an area variance allows deviation from dimensional requirements.
